name: Ottoman Empire
note: Claimed the Caliphate title after conquering Mamluk Egypt (1517). Unlike earlier Turkic states, developed a distinct Turkic-Islamic identity rather than Persianizing. Ruled Orthodox Christians, Catholics, Jews, and Arabs as separate millets (religious communities) — religion defined the subject's legal identity, not ethnicity

figures.name: Osman I
figures.significance: The dynasty is named after him (Osmanli)

figures.name: Mehmed II (Fatih)
figures.significance: Conquered Constantinople (1453); ended the Byzantine Empire; claimed Kayser-i Rum

figures.name: Suleiman the Magnificent
figures.significance: Zenith of the Ottoman Empire; also the Lawgiver who reformed the legal system

figures.name: Mustafa Kemal Ataturk
figures.significance: Ended the Sultanate (1922) and Caliphate (1924); founded the Republic of Turkey
